primary_variables.matched_definition: Addition of metabolites or metabolic substrates to alter pathway activity.
evidence: investigated whether CRPS ameliorated metabolic abnormalities associated with MetS in middle-aged women
the CRPS group was asked to consume calorie-controlled lunches and Herbalife Formula 1® (Herbalife, Los Angeles, CA, USA) for dinner
Herbalife Formula 1® was provided as a protein supplement to increase the participants’ daily protein intake

attributes.dose_or_concentration: 25 g of the protein powder (comprising 11 g of carbohydrates, 0.6 g of fat, and 8 g of protein)
evidence: the CRPS group was asked to consume calorie-controlled lunches and Herbalife Formula 1® (Herbalife, Los Angeles, CA, USA) for dinner
the participants were asked to mix 25 g of the protein powder (comprising 11 g of carbohydrates, 0.6 g of fat, and 8 g of protein) with water (81 kcal/serving)
blood samples from qualified participants were drawn before and after the dietary treatment
The participants’ height, body weight, WC, and body composition were measured at baseline (week 0) and after intervention (week 12)

replicate_hints: seven and six subjects’ plasma samples were selected in CR and CRPS groups for the proteomic analysis
multiplex_channel_hints: Protein quantification was based on label-free quantitative analysis

evidence: relative plasma levels of alpha-2-macroglobulin (A2M), C4b-binding protein alpha chain (C4BPA), complement C1r subcomponent-like protein (C1RL), complement component C6 (C6), complement component C8 gamma chain (C8G), and vitamin K-dependent protein S (PROS) were significantly different between the CRPS and CR groups
blood samples from qualified participants were drawn before and after the dietary treatment
A comparison between the baseline and postintervention clinical and biochemical measurements between the groups was conducted

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment used a data-dependent acquisition (DDA) method on an LTQ-Orbitrap Elite and employed label-free quantitative analysis based on spectrum counting.
The experiment used a data-dependent acquisition (DDA) method on an LTQ-Orbitrap Elite and employed label-free quantitative analysis based on spectrum counting.
